mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding labor income and consumption capacity, which translates into higher corporate revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of PAYEMS trend signal is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day return frequency is not statistically greater than 50% (binomial p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.25%, the zero lower bound constraint on monetary policy triggers a portfolio rebalancing effect into equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of months when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) captures a broad demand-supply expansion that amplifies earnings growth expectations beyond either variable alone, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and INDPRO z-scores produces a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the signal underperforms the single-proxy RSAFS signal by more than 2% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) predicts positive S&P 500 returns during post-crisis recovery regimes because supply-led growth signals capacity utilization improvement and operating leverage, whereas it is irrelevant in late-cycle regimes.
failure_condition: During post-crisis recovery periods, INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or the signal's Sharpe ratio is negative over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) directly tracks consumer expenditure flows; sustained increases lift top-line revenue forecasts and drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS momentum produces a walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP (GDPC1) below zero triggers a recessionary discount in equities as earnings expectations collapse, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of quarters when GDPC1 growth is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) combined with a low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) compress real yields and force investors into equities as a hedge against negative real rates, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of low FEDFUNDS and rising CPIAUCSL produces negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of triggered instances, or the interaction term's t-statistic is below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) lead the construction cycle and employment in interest-rate sensitive sectors; month-over-month increases signal durable demand strength and predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST month-over-month increases are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.
